2606.06286.title: LLMs Can Leak Training Data But Do They Want To? A Propensity-Aware Evaluation of Memorization in LLMs
2606.06286.paper_summary: Large language models can reproduce training data, but existing memorization evaluations mostly measure whether models can be forced to do so, rather than whether they do so under ordinary use. We introduce PropMe, a propensity-aware framework for memorization evaluation that contrasts prefix-based capability attacks with non-adversarial evaluations. We propose a metric transformation that, applied to existing functions, allows to create propensity metrics. We further introduce SimpleTrace, a lightweight tracing pipeline built on infini-gram that deterministically attributes model generations to large-scale training corpora and computes verbatim, near-verbatim, and propensity-transformed memorization metrics. Evaluating two fully-open models: Comma and DFM Decoder on two datasets: Common Pile and Dynaword in two languages, we find a consistent gap between capability and propensity: prefix attacks elicit substantially stronger memorization signals than generic or dataset-specific prompts, while propensity scores remain low overall. Thus, the models can reveal training data when directly elicited, but rarely do so in more common non-adversarial settings. We also find that DFM Decoder, which is continually pre-trained from Comma, exhibits reduced memorization and memorization propensity for Common Pile, confirming that memorization capability can decrease when later training emphasizes partially different data. Our results suggest, and we encourage, that memorization audits should report both worst-case extractability and ordinary leakage propensity in order to have a more comprehensive view of this phenomenon.
2606.06286.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型可以重現訓練數據，但現有的記憶評估主要測量模型是否能被迫這樣做，而不是它們在普通使用中是否會這樣做。我們介紹了PropMe，一個考慮傾向的記憶評估框架，對比基於前綴的能力攻擊與非對抗性評估。我們提出了一種度量轉換，應用於現有函數，允許創建傾向度量。我們進一步介紹了SimpleTrace，一個基於infini-gram構建的輕量級追蹤管道，確定性地將模型生成歸因於大規模訓練語料庫，並計算逐字、近逐字和傾向轉換的記憶度量。我們在兩個數據集：Common Pile和Dynaword上，對兩個完全開放的模型：Comma和DFM Decoder進行評估，發現能力與傾向之間存在一致的差距：前綴攻擊引發的記憶信號明顯強於通用或特定數據集的提示，而傾向分數整體仍然較低。因此，這些模型在直接引發時可以揭示訓練數據，但在更常見的非對抗性環境中則很少這樣做。我們還發現，DFM Decoder，這是一個持續從Comma進行預訓練的模型，對Common Pile顯示出較低的記憶和記憶傾向，確認當後續訓練強調部分不同數據時，記憶能力可能會下降。我們的結果表明，我們鼓勵記憶審計應報告最壞情況的可提取性和普通泄露傾向，以便對這一現象有更全面的了解。
2606.06286.author: Gianluca Barmina et.al.
2606.06286.authors: Gianluca Barmina, Peter Schneider-Kamp, Lukas Galke Poech
